market: KOSPI faces historic volatility; record foreign sell-offs in late March (~30 trillion won) driven by Middle East tensions and concerns over new Google AI chip algorithms (Turboquant) impacting memory demand. Individual investors partially offset with record buying, but KOSPI remains globally weak.
art: Art market shows K-shaped recovery. Christie's Hong Kong saw a white-glove sale generating $83.8M (up 17% YoY), while Art Basel Hong Kong reported strong interest in Asian-Pacific artists. Market focus remains on established names and historical quality.
